title: Outcome Contract, Non-Goals, and Measures of Success · Product Reasoning Brief: Outcome Chain
summary: The proposed product promises engagement and delight but names no user decision, baseline, guardrail, accessibility outcome, or unacceptable harm. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp1 outcome chain increment in measurable outcome and non-goal contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Connect product capability to user behavior, user outcome, stakeholder outcome, and public consequence without assuming causality.
Synthesize patterns with source links, counterevidence, minority needs, uncertainty, and quotations short enough to protect identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Outcome Chain · Theory Outcome Chain
steps.instruction:
theory case — A team targets engagement growth without a baseline, causal path, burden guardrail, or threshold for stopping a harmful direction. The theory variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Outcome Chain contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Outcome Chain · Theory Outcome Chain.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for connect product capability to user behavior, user outcome, stakeholder outcome, and public consequence without assuming causality. For Predict Outcome Chain · Theory Outcome Chain, keep Outcome Chain connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

steps.title: Bound Outcome Chain · Theory Outcome Chain
steps.instruction:
theory case — A team targets engagement growth without a baseline, causal path, burden guardrail, or threshold for stopping a harmful direction. The theory variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Connect product capability to user behavior, user outcome, stakeholder outcome, and public consequence without assuming causality.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Outcome Chain application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.

Evidence target: Bound Outcome Chain · Theory Outcome Chain.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for connect product capability to user behavior, user outcome, stakeholder outcome, and public consequence without assuming causality. For Bound Outcome Chain · Theory Outcome Chain, keep Outcome Chain connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Connect product capability to user behavior, user outcome, stakeholder outcome, and public consequence without assuming causality. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Outcome Chain while producing measurable outcome and non-goal contract.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A team targets engagement growth without a baseline, causal path, burden guardrail, or threshold for stopping a harmful direction. The worked example variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Connect product capability to user behavior, user outcome, stakeholder outcome, and public consequence without assuming causality. A defensible solution applies Outcome Chain, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to measurable outcome and non-goal contract.

content.code:
# Worked focus: pp1-outcome-chain — Connect product capability to user behavior, user outcome, stakeholder outcome, and public consequence without assuming causality.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-4
focus: pp1-outcome-chain
decision-evidence: Connect product capability to user behavior, user outcome, stakeholder outcome, and public consequence without assuming causality.
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Shipping a capability directly proves the intended real-world outcome. This fails the Outcome Chain contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: SLSA specification (SLSA 1.2 approved 2025-11-06) — Controls source and build tracks, provenance, artifact identity, verification, and honest supply-chain claims.

content.label: Mastery evidence

steps.title: Repair the Outcome Chain defect · Theory Outcome Chain
steps.instruction:
theory case — A team targets engagement growth without a baseline, causal path, burden guardrail, or threshold for stopping a harmful direction. The theory variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Connect product capability to user behavior, user outcome, stakeholder outcome, and public consequence without assuming causality.

A teammate acts on this belief: “Shipping a capability directly proves the intended real-world outcome.” Which repair addresses the cause?

Evidence target: Repair the Outcome Chain defect · Theory Outcome Chain.

stimulus.title: Outcome Chain failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Shipping a capability directly proves the intended real-world outcome.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Connect product capability to user behavior, user outcome, stakeholder outcome, and public consequence without assuming causality.

options.text: Keep the Outcome Chain artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. That claim fails the repair the outcome chain defect evidence boundary for Outcome Chain.

For repair the outcome chain defect, apply Outcome Chain without its stated preconditions, omit the measurable outcome and non-goal contract trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Outcome Chain for measurable outcome and non-goal contract.
Reconstruct the conditions for Outcome Chain, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Defend the Outcome Chain model · Theory Outcome Chain
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Outcome Chain: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A team targets engagement growth without a baseline, causal path, burden guardrail, or threshold for stopping a harmful direction. The theory variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Connect product capability to user behavior, user outcome, stakeholder outcome, and public consequence without assuming causality.

Evidence target: Defend the Outcome Chain model · Theory Outcome Chain.
steps.why: Retrieving Outcome Chain through the concrete measurable outcome and non-goal contract case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the Outcome Chain model · Theory Outcome Chain, keep Outcome Chain connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Connect product capability to user behavior, user outcome, stakeholder outcome, and public consequence without assuming causality.
